strengths: Built and deployed production-grade agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators supporting real-world agent workflows — directly aligning with the need for non-prototype, tool-augmented multi-agent systems.

Proven track record scaling engineering systems under high-throughput conditions: optimized real-time data pipelines at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec), led event-driven GIS platform on Kubernetes, and architected low-latency DLang services — demonstrating deep backend and distributed systems expertise.

Demonstrated leadership in engineering culture and governance: established CI/CD, TDD, and pairing practices at RIDE Capital; founded open-source tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and led technical workshops — reflecting ability to build disciplined, observable, and evaluable engineering practices.
concerns: No explicit evidence of direct experience in fintech infrastructure (credit systems, working capital, trade decisioning) — core domain knowledge is inferred from adjacent systems, not stated.
Current role at re:cinq (April 2025–present) is listed as ongoing but the resume is dated in the future, raising minor credibility questions about timeline accuracy that require verification.

Limited public documentation of AI system evaluation pipelines, feedback loops, or regulatory compliance infrastructure — critical for auditable, finance-grade AI systems — though foundational skills (observability, testing) are strong.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, verifiable production experience building and operating real-world agentic AI systems and high-throughput distributed infrastructure — directly matching the CTO role’s core demands — while consistently demonstrating leadership in engineering rigor, observability, and team culture through open-source tooling, workshops, and scalable system design, making him a compelling fit despite domain-specific gaps that can be bridged through onboarding.
